name: cloud-sync
tagline: Sets up and checks claude-mem's cloud sync to cmem.ai Pro: migrates legacy tokens, retires the old sync daemon, restarts the worker.
verdict: Solid credential hygiene: token never hits argv/logs/stdout, is merged into JSON without clobbering other settings, and the file is chmod 600. Two things to weigh: the token is collected by pasting it into chat (AskUserQuestion), so it lands in the conversation transcript/session log outside the script's control; and step 4 kills the legacy sync daemon and r

highlights.summary: Step 4 kills the legacy daemon (`kill "$LEGACY_PID"`) and archives (`mv ... .retired`) its files automatically once ~/.claude-mem/cloud-sync.pid is found, with no AskUserQuestion confirmation before t

Step 3(b) explicitly asks the user, via AskUserQuestion, to paste their sync token and user id ('Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user to paste two values from cmem.ai -> Connect'). Script-side handling
No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g. when NOT to invoke this skill, such as for non-cloud/local-only memory questions).
